mnemonics.title: GREEN IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: The 4 Pillars of Green IT: ENERGY (Energy Efficiency), ECOLOGY (Environmental Protection), ECONOMICS (Cost Savings), and EMERGENCE (Innovation for Sustainability)

mnemonics.title: Data Center Efficiency
mnemonics.content: POWER: PUE Optimization With Effective Resources - Focus on reducing PUE through cooling, power distribution, and lighting efficiency

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency; lower is better (ideal is 1.0)
Power management technique adjusting CPU voltage/frequency based on workload to save energy
Rating system for evaluating environmental impact of electronic products
UN agency that develops standards for Green IT and sustainable ICT
Framework for corporate sustainability reporting including IT environmental impacts
Method to evaluate environmental impacts of products from cradle to grave
Includes purchase cost + operational costs including energy and disposal costs
Company providing cloud computing services with sustainability considerations

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pizza - Total pizza energy is facility energy (including kitchen, lights), and the pizza toppings are IT energy. PUE = Whole pizza ÷ Toppings. Better efficiency = thinner crust (less non-IT energy)

Think of a building with many empty rooms (underutilized servers). Virtualization is like converting single-occupancy rooms into multi-occupancy apartments, fitting more 'tenants' in the same building using less energy for lighting and heating.
Picture a ticking time bomb where each layer represents toxic elements (lead, mercury, cadmium). Without proper recycling, this bomb 'explodes' into soil and water contamination.

Use a glass filling analogy - Traditional servers are glasses that are only 15-30% full (utilized). Virtualization is like pouring all glasses into fewer completely full glasses, saving space and resources.

analogies.analogy: Like a bus carrying only one passenger vs. a bus with 50 passengers - virtualization fills the 'bus' (server) more efficiently, reducing the number of buses needed and fuel (energy) consumed.

Like a 'green building' with solar panels, rainwater harvesting, and natural ventilation - a green data center uses renewable energy, efficient cooling, and smart design to minimize environmental impact.

Like a dietary tracker for the planet - just as calories track food intake, carbon footprint tracks greenhouse gas emissions, and just as dieting reduces calorie intake, Green IT reduces carbon 'intake' into the atmosphere.

Like a submarine vs. an aircraft carrier - thin clients are lightweight 'submarines' that use minimal energy, while traditional PCs are 'aircraft carriers' that consume more resources for the same computational 'cargo'.
Like organ donation for electronics - just as organ donation gives life to others, e-waste recycling gives 'life' to materials by recovering valuable resources and preventing toxic 'pollution'.
